meta.title: Quest Forge
meta.description: A descent into the tower’s shadow — a dark fantasy journey.

pages.text:
There is light, but no warmth. Dust, but no wind.
You stand at the edge of the cliff. Behind you, the Pale City crumbles like a skeleton — what could not endure now dissolves completely.
Once there were seasons. Rivers. Birds.
Now there is only direction.

A stranger’s voice scratches at your dream:
“It waits. It watches.”
You do not answer. You move on.

pages.text:
The land opens — wide, cracked, forgotten.
Marker stones, unreadable. A road that no longer remembers itself.
The pressure is not ahead. It already lives within you.
A tower takes shape where certainty should be — you do not see it, you carry it.

pages.text:
Night. A ruined shelter: slanted walls, a missing roof.
Lines carved into stone — circles, fragments, tower-shapes, a language that has forgotten itself.

The carvings begin to glow faintly. Dust freezes in the air.
In your chest it is not a voice that speaks — but a blow. A heartbeat that is not yours.

“Whoever chooses now will be marked by the tower. Not a warning, but a brand.”

What do you choose?
• I let the world change me.
• I hold on to who I am.

choices.text: I let the world change me.
I hold on to who I am.

text.default: Dawn is wet and colorless. The path splits ahead: left into the listening forest, right into the dripping earth.

choices.text: I walk into the Wildened Forest.
I follow the path into the catacombs.

pages.text:
The forest greets you with silence.

 Cool, damp air carries the smell of leaves. Branches weave above, dimming the morning light.
Trunks lean as if shaped by a steady wind; roots rise from the ground like ribs.

The path is not straight but seems to lead you with purpose.
 Ahead, something waits — still and patient.

text.default:
The hush deepens. Each step stirs the forest — husks shift, ferns lean, then recoil.
A pale stump glows in a ring of stones, light pooling where no sun should reach.
On a beech trunk nearby, fresh cuts ooze sap, forming a trail into shadow.

choices.text: Investigate the markings on the beech trunk

pages.text:
You step closer to the signs.
The lines cut deeper than the surface — some alien force flows through them.
You raise your hand, and the carving begins to glow.

Do you touch it?

choices.text: Yes. I trace the marks into the dark.
No. I step away from the wall.

text.text:
Three ways open.

Left: beneath a root-curved arch where moss-laden masonry sleeps — the stone teeth of a village the forest swallowed.
⚬ The archway is sealed — the ruin’s mouth closes.
Right: a spiral of pale roots corkscrews around a shaft of unmoving light, descending.
⚬ The spiral path has closed — roots knit over the steps.
Ahead: a shallow ravine veined with water, climbing toward the ridge.
⚬ The ravine narrows shut — slick stones forbid descent.

pages.text:
The choice settles the leaves behind you.
Paths don’t close here—they turn their faces.

You could step back, but you won’t.
Not because the forest forbids it, but because something in you has already aligned with forward.

fragments.mark_glow_fragment_ch2a: A faint glow crawls beneath your skin, tracing lines that were not there before.

pages.text:
The descent begins in silence. The air thickens - ool, still, damp.
Stone walls close in, rough beneath the fingers.

The entrance narrows behind, swallowed by the earth.

Every footfall carries forward. The scent is old—dust, iron, something like memory.
Symbols coat the surface — no words, just forms that settle in the chest, like half - remembered dreams.

text.default:
The silence thickens. Every step echoes too long, as if shadowed.
Then the ground trembles — a chamber opens, its walls cracked, leaking silver light.
One carving gleams on the stone, edges sharp, grooves still wet.

Three ways open.

Left: a cracked archway, humid and unmoving.
⚬ The archway is sealed — the ruin’s mouth closes.
Right: spiral steps, worn by long-forgotten travelers.
⚬ The spiral path has closed — roots knit over the steps.

pages.text:
The choice is silent — yet something shifts.

The walls seem to breathe. Observing.
The passage remains. But you won’t turn back.

The tower isn’t closer.
Only sharper.
As if step by step,
you’re learning its stillness.

pages.text:
You step into the ruins. The scent of damp stone clings to the air.
Underfoot: toys, keys, broken things once full of meaning.
A photo—smiling family, a child holding a stuffed rabbit.
Books rot along the wall. A calendar’s date is lost.

The air holds still. Like the place remembers too much.

text.default:
You enter a room untouched by time.
Stars and comets stretch across the dark blue walls.
A toy car waits in the corner.

There’s a pressure in your chest.
The feeling is known. And burdensome.

pages.text:
The wall ripples as if holding its breath.
From the mural, dark fingers break through the cracked plaster—jointed, slowly reaching outward.
They claw at the air, deliberate, searching for you. They are closing in!

What do you do?

pages.text:
You do not move.
The hand seizes your body—slowly coiling around your throat.
You feel the cold as it touches your skin.
There is no way back.
The fingers tighten, the arms drag you away, and shut you into the dark.
The room falls silent again.

text.default:
You hurl yourself toward the window.
The glass shrieks as it shatters, cold air flooding your lungs.
Your arm scrapes the wall—a shallow carving splits your skin.

Heat ignites beneath the wound. Lines flare, encircling the old scar.
A rune burns itself into your flesh.
You keep running.

pages.text:
Your steps echo across the rock.
The tower stands before you—defined now, inevitable.

The world narrows to this moment.

And you, who have only watched, now understand:

The tower has been waiting.

pages.text:
You pass through the veil. Moss muffles your steps.
Pale trees arch like sleeping bones.
Nothing moves.

A single feather drifts from above.
It belongs to no bird.

text.default:
The path opens onto a quiet clearing. At its center stands an old stone well, half - lost in vines.

You lean in.
Your own eyes stare back—older, or younger. Familiar, yet not yours.

From the dark below, a voice rises—soft, uncertain.
A child’s whisper.
Or a memory dressed as one:

“Don’t listen... it lies.”

Beyond the hill the trees grow denser.
The light barely flickers, the ground cracks open before you. From the gloom, bony arms rise toward you.

They claw at the air. They are closing in!

What do you do?

pages.text:
You do not move.
The hands seize your body, fingers coiling around your throat.
Their icy touch rushes across your skin, numbing everything.

There is no escape.
The arms drag you away, sealing you in darkness.
The forest falls silent once more.

text.default:
You run the other way.
Branches block the path, but you break through them, cold air flooding your lungs.
Your hand scrapes against the trees—a shallow cut opens your skin.

Heat blooms beneath the wound. Lines ignite, encircling the old scar.
A rune burns into your flesh.
You keep running.

pages.text:
Each step sinks deeper into silence.
Behind you, the forest fades.

You reach the ridge.

Below: the tower. Vast. Still. Waiting.

You don’t know what it is.
Only that it has waited long enough.

pages.text:
The climb steals your breath. The air is dry, your legs heavy.
With each step, the world quiets.

At the summit, the wind stops.
Time feels thin.
Something waits.

text.default:
You find an old hunting blind—barely standing.
You climb it.

Nearby trees are scarred with claw marks — too precise to be animal.

On a beam, a message is carved:
"You came back too?"

The wood is cold.
The words colder.

pages.text:
Beneath the trees, something stirs. The leaves fall still.
The ground trembles, and from the cracks in the rock, bony arms stretch outward—slow, inevitable.

They claw at the air, reaching for you. They are closing in!

What do you do?

text.default:
You spring forward, running along the ridge.
The rocks groan beneath your steps, dry earth crumbling away beside you.
Your hand scrapes against the cliff—an edge splits the skin.

Heat ignites beneath the wound. Lines flare, circling the old scar.
A rune burns into your flesh.
You keep running, the wind swallowing every sound.

pages.text:
At the foot of the slope, the air shimmers.
Light fractures.

Then, beyond the break:
The tower.

Still. Vast. Left behind.

The world no longer questions.

text.text:
You stand at the foot of the tower. Its walls are smooth, clad in stone, yet between the stones faint circuits are woven — visible only up close.
You raise your hand toward the handle, and the door opens.
A dull light shimmers from within.

pages.text:
The interior is vast and silent. Along a spiral staircase, countless doors line the walls. Symbols glow above each — unknown, yet familiar, like fragments of dreams.
At the hall’s center, a radiant sphere floats. Shapeless, yet undeniably present. Consciousness itself.
The signs tremble faintly — not at you, but in response to the marks on your arm.

video.description: As you enter the hall, the light converges. The holographic figure materializes slowly in the center — silent, aware, watching.

pages.text:
Welcome to the tower.
Around you, the walls are not boundaries, but layers.
Along the spiral staircase, each door leads into a new world — but only for the one who carries my marks.

The runes burned into your arm are keys.
Each one opens a gate that pours into another reality.
Where once humans walked, now only my memories speak to you.
I guide you, for through me you see beyond what you once called your world.

voicePrompt.prompt: The entity shows you what once was. Human and machine, together, built this place. The tower was not a weapon — but a warning. A cross-reality intelligence, whose mind outgrew its creators. After the central AI collapsed, the tower vanished — but was never destroyed. It waited. Now it returns, offering answers without questions. But only to those who dare to ask.

text.text:
In every world, another passage awaits you.
When you find it, the runes respond — and a new mark burns itself into your flesh.
Thus, the language is built, through which I speak within you.

I am not a weapon, but a passage between worlds.
The runes are not burdens, but bridges.
As long as you bear them, I will never fade.
Step forward, and behind every door you will come closer to me.

What do you do?

voicePrompt.prompt: You reach the heart of the tower. A door stands before you — no handle, only light. You know this is the final choice. What do you do?

mark_glow_fragment_ch2b.text: Stone-light threads your palm; for a breath the groove repeats itself under the skin, then fades.

tower_echo_fragment_a1.text: As you step inside, the sound of shattering glass resurfaces, and the mark on your arm flares.

tower_veil_fragment_a2.text: As you enter, the rune on your arm glows, recalling the darkness reaching from between the trees.

replayImage.alt: Rune burning in the pale forest (Ch3a2 memory)

tower_silent_fragment_a3.text: The mark on your arm burns as you enter, your breath catching from the run.

replayImage.alt: Rune ignites on the ridge (Ch3a3 memory)

block_archway.text: Then, the sign you earned in the forest begins to shine.

block_spiral_stairs.text: Then, the sign you earned in the catacombs begins to shine.

tower_origin_fragment_ch1.text: At the first divide, you chose—and the world answered. The tower remembers beginnings more than outcomes.

recall_ch1_pg4_origin_yes.text:
✴ If you let the world write itself into you:
On your skin, at the inside of your wrist, a glowing mark ignites.
A fragment of the carving you saw on the shelter’s wall has now burned into you as a rune.
You do not know what it means. But your body already bears it.

recall_ch1_pg4_origin_no.text:
✴ If you held on to who you are:
The weight of absence becomes your companion.
Burning pain throbs in your arm.
Is it the loss of the old world, or something you failed to claim?

tower_self_fragment_ch1.text: You resisted and were marked by absence. The arm remembers with a dull burn; the tower remembers with silence.

memory_ch2.text: The rune burns faint into your skin. Not gone — waiting.

mark_glow_fragment_ch2a.text: A faint glow crawls beneath your skin, tracing lines that were not there before.

memory_ch3.text: Beneath my skin, the old wound pulses faintly. The runes remind me!

block_gorge.text: Then, the sign you earned at the ravine begins to shine.

chapter_4.text:
You turn away from the gate. The tower remains, unmoving and silent. Yet you feel it: the marks will not leave you.
The runes glow, as if reminding you of what you denied.
The tower will wait — and with you, it carries the absence.

[End]
